question_text: Which statement correctly describes what happens to temperature and day length in the Northern Hemisphere as it moves from autumn towards winter?

options.label: A
options.text: Both day length and temperature increase as winter approaches

options.label: B
options.text: Day length decreases and temperature decreases as winter approaches

options.label: C
options.text: Day length increases but temperature decreases as winter approaches

options.label: D
options.text: Day length decreases but temperature increases as winter approaches

correct_answer: B) Day length decreases and temperature decreases as winter approaches
explanation: As autumn turns to winter, the Northern Hemisphere tilts further away from the Sun. This causes days to become shorter and the Sun's rays to strike at a lower angle, delivering less energy and causing temperatures to fall.
